dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8120 IH: Veterans Benefits Information Protection Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Pappas (for himself and Mr. Bacon) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce

form.official-title: To amend the Communications Act of 1934 to restrict, with respect to calls made to telephone numbers assigned to a Federal department or agency, the use of certain automated telephone equipment, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Veterans Benefits Information Protection Act.

section.header: Restrictions on the use of automated telephone equipment
section.text: Section 227(b)(1) of the Communications Act of 1934 (47 U.S.C. 227(b)(1)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (C), by striking or at the end;
in subparagraph (D), by striking the period at the end and inserting ; or; and
by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: to make any call to a telephone number assigned to a Federal department or agency if such call is made by means of equipment that—

clause.text: has the capacity, without further human intervention after initial activation—

subclause.text: to make a series of repeated calls to such number; and
to provide and receive information by means of such calls; and

clause.text: is operated by any individual or entity other than the individual or entity to whom the information provided or received during such call relates.
